mission.title: Install and Configure Oscal Compass on Kubernetes
mission.description: Compliance Trestle is a tooling platform designed to manage compliance as code using NIST's OSCAL standard. Installing it allows you to create, validate, and govern compliance documentation efficiently within your CI/CD pipelines.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for Compliance Trestle to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Apply the Deployment Manifest
steps.description: Deploy Compliance Trestle using the provided Kubernetes manifests.

steps.title: Apply the Service Manifest
steps.description: Expose Compliance Trestle using a service.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check if the pods are running correctly in the Compliance Trestle namespace.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Compliance Trestle pods running in the 'compliance' namespace, and you will be able to access the application via http://localhost:8080. Resource limits will be applied to the deployment.

uninstall.title: Remove Compliance Trestle Resources
uninstall.description: Delete the resources created for Compliance Trestle.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Compliance Trestle
upgrade.description: To upgrade Compliance Trestle, apply the new version of the manifests.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the pod is not starting, check the logs for errors using:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n compliance-trestle
```

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not accessible, ensure that the correct ports are exposed and check the service configuration:
```bash
kubectl describe service <service-name> -n compliance-trestle
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If pods are in a pending state, it may be due to insufficient resources. Check the resource requests and limits set in the deployment manifest.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
